name: Grafana Mimir API
what_it_does: Open-source, horizontally scalable Prometheus-compatible metrics backend with REST API — providing multi-tenant metrics storage, Prometheus remote_write ingestion, PromQL querying, and rule evaluation at any scale, as the open-source core of Grafana Cloud metrics.
use_cases: Storing and querying Prometheus metrics at scale via Prometheus-compatible API
Running multi-tenant metrics infrastructure for different teams via tenant isolation
Querying long-term metrics retention via PromQL API for historical analysis
Replacing Cortex with Mimir for improved performance and operational simplicity
Using Grafana Cloud metrics (powered by Mimir) via managed service
not_for: Log management (use Grafana Loki for logs)
Distributed tracing (use Grafana Tempo for traces)
Teams not using Prometheus or PromQL
best_when: A platform team needs horizontally scalable, multi-tenant Prometheus-compatible metrics storage with operational simplicity and the option for managed deployment via Grafana Cloud.
avoid_when: You need all-in-one observability without separate components, prefer VictoriaMetrics simplicity, or don't use Prometheus.

auth.notes: Multi-tenant auth via X-Scope-OrgID header for tenant identification. No auth in single-tenant mode. Grafana Cloud (managed Mimir) uses API keys for authentication. Reverse proxy recommended for auth in self-hosted deployments.

pricing.paid_tiers: Grafana Cloud managed: from $0.01/series/month; Enterprise support available
pricing.notes: Open-source Apache 2.0. Self-hosted free. Grafana Cloud managed option with usage-based pricing. Swedish company (Grafana Labs, Stockholm). CNCF Sandbox project. Successor to Cortex (which Grafana Labs also contributed to). Used in Grafana Cloud metrics infrastructure.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — configurable per tenant; Grafana Cloud has per-plan limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Prometheus-compatible HTTP API for Querier, Ingester, and Admin components. Multi-tenant via X-Scope-OrgID header. Good documentation from Grafana Labs. Grafana Cloud (managed) has simpler auth than self-hosted. No webhooks. Self-hosted endpoint varies per deployment.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All metric query operations fully idempotent. Remote write ingestion idempotent for same timestamp/series. Admin operations idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: X-Scope-OrgID header required for multi-tenant deployments
No default auth in self-hosted — add reverse proxy for security
Multi-component architecture similar to Thanos — requires operational knowledge
Object storage (S3, GCS) required for long-term metrics persistence

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable. No default auth — tenant isolation via X-Scope-OrgID (not cryptographic). Grafana Cloud adds authentication layer. Open-source Apache 2.0. Self-hosted data control. Object storage credentials must be secured separately. Grafana Labs SOC2 certified for cloud offering.
